did.unittitle: Ginny Crouch Stanford collection of Frank Stanford

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains writings, correspondence, notebooks, photographs, artwork, personal papers, audiovisual materials, and other papers by or related to American poet Frank Stanford. Includes material related to painter Ginny Crouch Stanford, including her artwork and correspondence.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jeff Maser on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2014.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Ginny Crouch Stanford Collection of Frank Stanford. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Ginny Crouch Stanford Collection of Frank Stanford is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

Information included in the Content Description note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
Description, including lists of Stanford's works, was provided by Ginny Crouch Stanford at the time of acquisition, and has been repurposed to describe folder contents.

bioghist.head: Frank Stanford
bioghist.p: Frank Stanford (1948- 1978) was an American poet. He authored over ten books of poetry, including The Singing Knives (1972), Ladies from Hell (1974), Field Talk (1974), Shade (1975), Arkansas Bench Stone (1975), Constant Stranger (1976), The Battlefield Where the Moon Says I Love You (1977), and Crib Death (1978). His posthumous collection, What About This: Collected Poems of Frank Stanford (2015), was a finalist for the National Books Critics Circle Award.

Stanford was born Francis Gildart Smith in Richton, Mississippi. In 1974 he married the painter Ginny Crouch, and they moved to Eureka Springs, Arkansas, where Stanford worked as a land surveyor. In 1975, Stanford began a relationship with poet C. D. Wright which would last the rest of Stanford's life. In 1976, Stanford founded Lost Roads Publishers, an independent literary publisher that produced books of poetry, literary fiction, and translation.
At the age of twenty-nine, on June 3, 1978, Stanford died of self-inflicted gunshot wounds.

Ginny Crouch Stanford is a painter and contributed artwork to accompany many of Stanford's bodies of poetry, including works produced for Mill Mountain Press, Lost Roads Publications and other periodicals. Following Stanford's death, Crouch Stanford, along with C. D. Wright as co-executors of his estate, played instrumental roles in preserving his legacy.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material is open for research.
Boxes 13 and 15 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 14 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Frank Stanford papers (YCAL MSS 295), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven series: Series I: Writings, 1957 - 1979, Series II: Correspondence, 1970 - 2009, Series III: Personal papers, 1948 - 1998, Series IV: Artwork, 1973 - 1975, Series V: Photographs, 1950 - 1991, Series VI: Audiovisual materials, 1957, 1974, Series VII: Electronic media, undated

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains notebooks that include drawings, poem drafts, titled and untitled poems, and prose works. The notebooks also include notes on film and drawings. Poems in this series include titles from the works Arkansas Bench Stone , Approacheth the Ship and Wonder , Constant Stranger , Crib Death , Field Talk , Ladies From Hell , Let Him Lay There , St. Francis and the Wolf , and Shade .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by folder title.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Description, including lists of Stanford's works, have been provided by Ginny Crouch Stanford at the time of acquisition, and have been repurposed to describe folder contents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 49 published poems, entirely in photocopy, with the exception of the last poem. Two page table of contents with note by Stanford (in photocopy) indicating that underlined poem titles, "denote published poems which have not appeared in pamphlet." The poems listed on the contents page are not all present, with gaps in the pagination.

scopecontent.p: BEN FALLOW'S TALE. 57 pages and cover sheet. Short story. A photocopy of the original typescript. Over thirty small handwritten changes and additions. These changes were incorporated into the published version. CULPA. Also included is another copy. 57 pages and front and rear cover sheet. Short story. Last unnumbered leaf provides an alternate title, "Legend of a Poet." A copy made of the above.

widow
nightingale
the Negro child with the dipnet in one fist and the match in the other
THE DOUBLE SUICIDE OF THE MIRROR AND THE ROSE
THE MOON
exile
so many things keep moving this instant
death of the french kiss and the hall of mirrors
fade away clear light in the roots of the Chickasaw wilderness
furious ballad of courting at the ice house
gnats
the shadow of a character standing behind one of the seven pillars of wisdom
THE VOYAGERS
evening the priest
the black ship youth who went down without me
she cleaned the dust from a mirror and ate strawberries
a gazebo of death and blue rosemary
was
day after tomorrow
the afternoon of another hunter
vanish
THE BURIED SWORD
from The Battlefield Where The Moon Says I Love You (four pages)
strut
eloping boy
here rattler here
nine men from the hills tuning the mandolins of silence
nobody is going to touch me
the rings
some shade
throat
no more legends
a safari of the spirit by water
the molested child goes to the dark tower again 140 years to the day
mascara
irons
the shepherd boy and the moon
Isinglass
Canephorae
from THE SNAKE DOCTORS (three pages)
KEEPING THE LORD'S NIGHT WATCH

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Green Royal Vernon Line Compositions notebook, 104 pages. Student composition book. Drawings, notes, and prose on eighteen sides. Stanford drawing of Ginny Crouch Stanford asleep beneath a window. Notes for a film, which may be in part for It Wasn't A Dream, It Was A Flood. Beginnings of prose works.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black Square Deal Composition, 120 pages with writing on three sides only. Contents include lists of titles and names.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black unlined Vernon Royal Composition Book, 124 pages. Contents include drawings, prose notes, and poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Green Royal Vernon Line Compositions, 116 pages. Student composition book. Contents include poems, prose, and drawings. Sixteen sides. Titled poems include "The Blue Canoe," "I Walk These Hills," and "Blues for a Gentleman." The remaining text is prose with a poem fragment. On the last two leaves, and inside the rear cover, are ink drawings by Stanford.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: BUSCH / SPRING 1973." notebook. "Ginny crouch / busch arkansas" penned inside the front cover. Contents description for this notebook by Ginny Stanford: "Fifty drawings by GS at the direction of FS. One drawing by FS in brown ink. Nine pages of notes written by GS at the direction of FS - list of first lines: From blackberry tales, Plan elderly man - girl in breakfast room, Musical, horses, beggars, snake priest Elderly man with mustache from milk on mouth, Negroes giving the finger to police car A bar/restaurant sparsely furnished with, No more legends, Portrait of Frank, Delacroix, Diablo, The Painter and the Poet, etc. on the back page."

did.unittitle: Notebook: notes and first drafts of poems

Two page typescript on yellow legal sheets, first line: "While everyone in the state of Arkansas was paying two bits..."

Four handwritten pages of notes notes in brown ink on ivory legal paper. Notes from Ginny Crouch: from 1973 – references to Richard Banks of Eureka Springs, the Joy Motel in Eureka Springs, Bill and Bertha B; and the painting of Richard by GS.These notes possibly in connection with It Wasn't A Dream, It Was A Flood].

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All of the below poems are photocopies of typescripts, corrected typescripts, or published texts. Though there are no original annotations, occasionally there are editorial notes that appear only here, almost none of which are in Stanford's hand. Poems include:

KITE TIED TO A TOMBSTONE

the dream children

the troupe (nine pages)

the ambush

THE SHEPHERD BOY AND THE MOON
BREAKING THROUGH THE SKIN
ONE FINGER ZEN
DEATH AND MEMORY
ISINGLASS

A SAFARI OF THE SPIRIT BY WATER

ballad of Roscoe Spencer
The Long Dark Night

A man in a field saw a she-wolf

RIVERLIGHT
FINE HORSES PUT DOWN AT MORNING

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems and prose, including:

list.item: Dante Gagriel [sic] Rossetti Dreams he was a child again / The summer light. Two single carbon typescripts, each complete, but significantly different, both with handwritten changes
GOING LAME WITH THE SOUTH WIND FROM THE NIGHT AGAIN.Two page carbon typescript, with a handwritten dedication
THE DREAM typescript
THE BLOOD BOOT. Two copies, one a single page typescript, the next a two page version, signed by Stanford at the conclusion. In addition, a carbon of the second version
BAR typescript
The night ride. Photocopy of typescript
Corrected typescript, first line: "In the bow was a young mother..."
DREAM FUGUE typescript

SOME POEMS WHO DREAMED THEY WERE MANDOLINS AND DARK BREAD, 1959. Three page carbon typescript. Three minor handwritten corrections and the addition, "These poems are from the manuscript: The Gypsy's Nightmare and Daughters I Loved in the Fire." Poems under this heading include "evening the priest," "she cleaned the dust from a mirror and ate strawberries," "a gazebo of death and blue rosemary," "was," "day after tomorrow," "that afternoon of another hunter, " and "vanish"
QUICK AND THE DEAD. Typescript. In the upper corner "Ms. GONE / Draft Final / Ginny S." A few handwritten notes and corrections

Fade away clear light in the roots of the Chickasaw wilderness. Two page photocopy of typescript, with the handwritten addition by Frank Stanford, "I call a group of these: Poems Buried In the Moon Lake Levee 1961." Includes the short works, "I went to bed and astonished what was sung," "furious ballad of courting at the ice house," "death of the french kiss and the hall of mirrors," "and she wept and then she spoke"
From Dream Fugue. Two carbon typescripts, numbered 115 and 27. Two minor handwritten corrections
Tryst. Three page typescript with the holograph addition, "1961 / from Moss and the Body." Together with a full carbon copy and two pages of a second carbon copy
Original typescript, first line: "Midsummer, 1950, aboard ship, somewhere off the coast of Chile, bound for home port..."
Prose fragment, concluded, "Midwinter, 1965, in a wild drive in Midnight, Mississippi"
Silence this maze. Single page prose typescript. Several small handwritten changes and additions. Penned along the top by FS, "P.23 Blue Yodel of Poets of Times Past"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

This Conflict
Slow Rag of the Yearbook (two pages)

Liaison (two pages)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 20 pages, cover sheet, and an additional typewritten first page. Typescript, each page bearing extensive handwritten corrections and changes.

did.unittitle: "The Yacht Which Has Yet to See Water" typescript
"The Yacht That Has Yet to See Water" two typescripts (photocopy)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poetry and fiction fragments. Complete text of You, as published by Mill Mountain Press, 1979.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains personal correspondence, including correspondence with Stanford's family and peers in the poetry and writing community.
Correspondence includes material related to academic and mainstream presses, and Seventeen Magazine, as well as correspondence written to Crouch Stanford after Stanford's death.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by correspondent's last name.

did.unittitle: Crouch, Ginny: letters to Crouch Stanford after Stanford's death

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains biographical material including court documents relating to Stanford's adoption, the Stanford family genealogy and family history, documents relating to his death, and other personal memorabilia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains court documents and other materials relating to Stanford's adoption, the Stanford family genealogy, and family history. Includes a genealogical chart for the Rogers-Stanford families.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder includes Stanford's birth certificate, library cards, ticket stubs, insurance cards, drivers licenses, business cards, and blank stationary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a funeral record, death certificate, and police report relating to Stanford's death. Includes letters by Stanford detailing funeral instructions, dedications to unpublished manuscripts, dispersal of estate, and other end-of-life matters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains ink drawings by Stanford, in addition to a sketchbook containing Crouch Stanford's drawings, as well as Stanford's poems, fiction, prose, and cinematography notes. The drawings are studies for a work that would appear in Stanford's collection Shade . Artwork includes twenty ink drawings by Crouch Stanford, composed at the direction of Stanford for Mill Mountain Press. Includes drawings from Shade , Ladies from Hell , and Arkansas Bench Stone . This series also contains three posters featuring drawings by Crouch Stanford. The posters advertise Stanford's books Arkansas Bench Stone , Ladies From Hell , and Shade.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2 inch black hardcover commercial unlined sketchbook containing poems, fiction, prose, and cinematography notes by Stanford. Throughout the sketchbook are ink drawings of Stanford by Crouch Stanford. The drawings are studies for Shade .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ginny Crouch Stanford indicated this folder contains, "Twenty ink drawings by GS, composed at the direction of FS for Mill Mountain Press 1973-1975. One sketch for Theory of Relativity on page with notes GS wrote down at the direction of FS."

Contents include four drawings from Shade , all nine drawings from Ladies From Hell , plus an additional image, unused, a variation with an additional figure. One of the two drawings from Arkansas Bench Stone . Of the remaining five drawings, four are detailed portraits of film directors, the fifth a character hanging out below a marquee. Likely to accompany a version of Stanford's "Automatic Co-Pilot."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three 13 x 10 inch posters, produced to advertise books by Stanford. Each features a reproduction of a drawing by Crouch Stanford. Includes Arkansas Bench Stone: Poems by Frank Stanford, Neosho: Mill Mountain Press [1975]. Ladies from Hell, Neosho: Mill Mountain Press [1974]. Shade: Poems by Frank Stanford, Neosho: Mill Mountain Press [1975].

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographic prints, slides and negatives from Stanford's chilhood, up until his death. Images depict Stanford, Ginny Crouch Stanford, and members of Stanford's circle of friends and family. Many of the photographs were taken by Crouch Stanford, and include images of Stanford during their time living in Arkansas.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged in the order it was received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes childhood photos of Stanford, and photos of Allen Ginsberg in Fayetteville.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes images of Stanford in Busch, and Rocky Branch, Arkansas, at Subiaco Abbey, in Duval, Missouri, and images of Stanford with his friends and family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes images of Stanford in Busch, Fayetteville, and Subiaco, Arkansas, and New Orleans. Includes photographs of paintings made by Crouch Stanford under the direction of Stanford, and Stanford during the making of It Wasn't a Dream, It Was a Flood .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images include Stanford in Busch, Arkansas, and New Orleans. Includes reference photographs of Stanford for paintings by Crouch Stanford, as well as images of her artwork. Other material includes contact sheets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Slides of Stanford posing on the porch at the Duval farmhouse, summer 1977, taken by Crouch Stanford.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains a 1/4 inch magnetic reel-to-reel tape annotated on the verso of the box, "Tape made by Ruth Stanford on her trip to Dallas and Colorado in July, 1957 addressed to her brother Frank."

Additional audiovisual materials include the original print of the 16mm film, It Wasn't a Dream, It Was a Flood, the autobiographical 25 minute film made by Stanford and Mill Mountain Press publisher Irv Broughton in 1974.

did.unittitle: Tape made by Ruth Stanford on her trip to Dallas and Colorado in July, 1957 Addressed to her brother Frank.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: It Wasn't a Dream, It Was a Flood: film

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Canister containing an original 16 mm print, one of only a handful produced. Autobiographical 25 minute film made by Stanford and Mill Mountain Press publisher Irv Broughton in 1974. Stanford appears in the film, as do friends upon whom recurring characters in Stanford's work were based. Winner of one of the Judge's Awards at the 1975 Northwest Film and Video Festival. Due to the lack of music clearances, this film has not had a theatrical release, though it has screened at the Frank Stanford Literary Festival, and on other occasions.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Reference copies of this item may only be consulted in the Beinecke Reading Room.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
